transcript:
[Meeting date: 2026-08-10]
[00:00] You: Vendor contract renewal planning. We have several coming up.
[00:40] Speaker 1: Our AWS contract renews November 1st. We need to submit the usage forecast to our account manager 45 days in advance — so by September 17th.
[01:20] Speaker 2: The Datadog contract renews October 15th. I'll send them the renewal request by September 15th.
[01:55] Speaker 3: Zendesk is up for renewal on December 1st. We're 30 days past the cancellation notice window, so we're locked in for another year regardless.
[02:35] Speaker 1: Stripe's contract doesn't expire — it's month-to-month. But we should renegotiate the processing rate since our volume grew. I'd like to get that negotiation done before end of quarter, so by September 30th.
[03:15] You: What about GitHub Enterprise?
[03:30] Speaker 2: GitHub Enterprise renews January 15th, 2027. We need to submit a seat count update by December 1st.
[04:05] Speaker 3: The biggest one is Salesforce — it comes up for renewal on October 31st. We've been in contract negotiations for a month. I'll get the final terms from their AE by August 21st.
[04:45] Speaker 1: We also have a new vendor evaluation in progress — we're looking at replacing Intercom. The vendor evaluation should be done as soon as possible, but realistically we have until end of year before we need to make a decision.
[05:20] You: Good. I'll also need budget approvals for all renewals over $50k. That approval cycle takes about 3 weeks, so I'll need proposals from each of you well in advance.

I'll send them the renewal request by September 15th.
I'd like to get that negotiation done before end of quarter, so by September 30th.

decisions: Zendesk is auto-renewed for another year — cancellation window already passed
Vendor evaluation for Intercom replacement needed before end of year

dates.description: AWS usage forecast due to account manager
